case_name: GEORGIA MOTON v. EMORY HEALTHCARE, INC.

summary.brief: GEORGIA MOTON v. EMORY HEALTHCARE, INC.. Medical malpractice case involving paediatrics.

evidence_index.text: Moton was admitted to Emory Hillandale Hospital for an altered mental state following an accidental overdose of prescribed Keppra.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: Medical personnel established four IV lines for the administration of medication, including three 20-gauge lines (left wrist, left antecubital area, and left side of neck) and one 22-gauge line (left hand).
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: On October 31, defendant White intravenously administered to Moton an initial loading dose of Dilantin, and on November 1, defendant Stokes intravenously administered a second dose of Dilantin.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: Medical staff did not document through which IV the medication was administered.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: Dr. Smith testified that the standard of care for administering Dilantin is through a large vein rather than a smaller vein such as the hand.
evidence_index.speaker: Dr. Smith

evidence_index.text: Dr. Liu testified that it is 'more likely than not, [the] Dilantin was infiltrated through the peripheral IV that was inserted in the left hand of Ms. Moton, resulting in the injuries that she sustained.'
evidence_index.speaker: Dr. Liu

evidence_index.text: The 'Discharge Documentation' prepared by hospitalist physician Nivedea Basu, noted the following: 'Appears she had IV infiltration [with] resulting left and/left digits tissue necrosis'; 'IV Infiltration/L Hand Tissue Necrosis [and] L Hand Cellulitis . . . appears to be attributed to IV infiltration of AED (IV Dilantin)'; and that Moton 'will need outpatient F/U for elective amputation[.]'
evidence_index.speaker: Dr. Basu

evidence_index.text: The trial court found that res ipsa loquitor does not apply in a malpractice suit and that Moton’s experts’ failure to point to any specific act of negligence rendered the experts’ opinions unreliable and inadmissible.
evidence_index.speaker: Trial court finding

evidence_index.text: Georgia law requires only that an expert state an opinion regarding proximate causation in terms stronger than that of medical possibility, i.e., reasonable medical probability or reasonable medical certainty.
evidence_index.speaker: Court reasoning

evidence_index.text: We reverse the order of the trial court granting summary judgment in favor of defendants.
evidence_index.speaker: Court conclusion

initial_state.chief_complaint: Altered mental state following accidental overdose
initial_state.history_of_present_illness: Patient admitted for altered mental state after accidental overdose of prescribed Keppra.

focused_exam.neurological: Patient sedated and on ventilator, unable to communicate

initial_state.initial_working_problem: Altered mental state due to medication overdose

requestables.name: Plastic Surgery Consult
requestables.description: Assessment of left hand tissue damage

reveal.result_summary: Presence of large bullae and evolving necrosis on left hand
reveal.clinical_significance: Indicates severe tissue damage likely due to extravasation

timeline_phases.name: Hospital Admission
timeline_phases.description: Patient admitted for altered mental state

decision_points.clinical_context: Administration of Dilantin to patient with multiple IV lines
decision_points.prompt: Through which IV line should Dilantin be administered?

options.description: Administer through large vein in neck
options.clinical_reasoning: Standard of care requires administration through a large vein to prevent extravasation

options.description: Administer through small vein in hand
options.clinical_reasoning: Increased risk of extravasation and tissue damage

actual_action_defendant.description: Defendant administered through small vein in hand

expected_action_court.description: Dilantin should have been administered through a large vein
expected_action_court.standard_of_care_basis: Expert testimony established this as standard

explanation.what_should_have_happened: Dilantin should have been administered through a large vein

patient_outcome.description: Patient developed severe tissue necrosis leading to amputation

key_findings: Breach of duty in administering Dilantin through small vein
Causation established - extravasation led to tissue necrosis

malpractice_determination.point_of_failure: Failure to administer Dilantin through appropriate IV line
malpractice_determination.counterfactual: Administration through a large vein would have prevented injury

factual_timeline.event: Patient admitted to Emory Hillandale Hospital

diagnoses.diagnosis: Tissue necrosis due to IV infiltration
